summary: Round-2 re-review: all three major round-1 findings are resolved and the suite is green under `go test -count=1 -race -p 1` (verified live, no race warnings). (1) Test_buildPrepare's dead loop is fixed — it now asserts `fmt.Sprintf("%d AS c%d", j, j)` per j, pins the expression count with `strings.Count(q0, " AS c")==planSize`, and adds a full-statement regexp; the litmus (a builder emitting duplicate `0 AS c0`) now fails the test. (2) The data race on the mid-run snapshot is eliminated — `mid` is now `atomic.Int64` (Store in goroutine, Load on main); confirmed clean under -race. (3) The monotonic-growth assertion is no longer vacuous — it asserts `final>=20` (concrete sustained-creation lower bound at Rate=50 over ~1s), `Positive(mid)`, and `mid<=final`; a single-increment loop would now fail. The minor D5 climax/first-Exec branches are covered by a faithful `fakeConn` (matches db.Conn exactly) in Test_runLoop_climaxAfterSuccess and Test_runLoop_firstExecError; D9 sanitize() has a dedicated pure-function table test plus a sentinel-leak assertion in the invalid-conninfo integration test; D8 application_name is asserted via SELECT current_setting. The only remaining gap is the documented `--show-memory` PG15 happy path (readBackendMemory has no container test), which is an acceptable known limitation — see below.

findings.issue: The Testing Strategy explicitly lists `--show-memory happy path on PG 15 (>=14): the memory read returns a value and the panel includes it` as an integration test, but no such test exists. readBackendMemory and the ShowMemory=true branch in runLoop (the memory read, the once-only warn path, and mem.Store) are exercised only manually. This is the single unaddressed item from round 1; the team has flagged it as a known gap and asked whether it is acceptable.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable as a known limitation, but cheap to close: in Test_runLoop_monotonicGrowth (or a sibling) set config.ShowMemory=true with a short ReportInterval (e.g. 100ms) against the existing PG15 container and, after runLoop returns, assert `mem.Load() > 0`. This covers readBackendMemory's happy path on PG15 with no new fixture. If left uncovered, document it alongside the already-noted PG<14 graceful-degradation manual limitation so both memory paths are explicitly accounted for.
